entities.name: DUMAS METHOD
entities.description: A rapid and precise technique for determining total carbon and nitrogen in soils involving high-temperature combustion.

entities.name: TOTAL CARBON
entities.description: The total amount of carbon present in soil samples, also referred to as TC.

entities.name: TOTAL NITROGEN
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen present in soil samples, also referred to as TN.

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The medium from which samples are taken for carbon and nitrogen estimation.

entities.name: HIGH-TEMPERATURE COMBUSTION
entities.description: The process of burning samples at high temperatures to evolve gases for detection.

entities.name: OXYGEN-RICH CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environmental state required for the combustion process within the Dumas method.

entities.name: TC-TN ANALYZERS
entities.description: Instruments used to detect evolved gases to measure total carbon and total nitrogen.

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients, analyzed through TC and TN measurements.

entities.name: NUTRIENT ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of determining the nutrient content of soil samples.
